claim_boundary: Retrospective, screen-relative directional evidence under a non-negative linear-combination model; not state conversion, functional rescue, or target validation.

dataset_context.system: donor-collapsed primary human CD4 perturbation-condition profiles
dataset_context.target: constructed external Th1-vs-Th2 direction from two source contrasts
not_measured: protein, cytokine, chromatin, durability, or phenotype rescue

updated_findings.finding: The cone beats mean, PCA-1, PCA-5, and the matched random-cone mean on the registered split; PCA-20, unconstrained least squares, and ridge score higher. Utility is constrained interpretability, not predictive superiority.
A circular held-out-to-in-sample ceiling claim was retired. The retained floor-to-synthetic-reachable-reference dynamic-range fraction is 0.463 under the primary uniform metric.
Rest is the strongest observed context. Stimulated held-out scores are weaker, and Stim48hr is sequencing-run confounded.

Cell-cycle ablation changes held-out cosine by about -0.002; the cone exceeds a norm-matched null; positive per-generator rescaling leaves the cone point invariant. These checks do not eliminate all confounding.
All 6,871 Rest generators were already source-flagged significant; restricting further to the top half by effect size improved held-out cosine from 0.4464 to 0.4580.
The directional ranking beats an effect-magnitude baseline on a curated 13-gene polarization panel. This is not full GRN validation.

Across 126 Norman K562 CRISPRa doubles, one retired legacy-v0 threshold label and 100 staged E-to-negative-E proxy labels flip. These are sensitivity diagnostics for removed heuristics, not mechanistic modality labels or validation of CD4 CRISPRi combinations.
The two external target sources have cosine 0.689 across 11,616 shared genes, with 68.5% sign concordance. Exact magnitude depends on target construction.

Of 25,672 unique genes in the union of target-source tables, 9,831 overlap the screen. The registered merged estimand is narrower: 11,616 genes are shared by both sources, 7,960 have concordant signs, and 6,188 remain after screen intersection. Thirty-eight of the 50 strongest target-table DE genes overlap the screen. Source-only held-out cosines are 0.438 for Ota and 0.342 for Hollbacher, versus 0.446 for the concordant merge.

open_requirements: grouped or module-level holdouts and claim-bearing structured nulls
paired measured CRISPRi and CRISPRa dictionaries
prospective validation from an established polarized starting state
